protocol: One LLM judge per dump (same as prior rounds): root-cause matching vs the old ReviewHog's 10 findings; every unmatched dump finding classified new_plausible/new_junk against the PR diff; NEW this round — each judge also assesses VALIDATOR VERDICT CORRECTNESS (the round's subject) and takes a position on the contested 'list_actions object-level access control' finding family. Judge calls are UNREVIEWED by a human. CROSS-JUDGE SPLIT, now 5-4 across both rounds (refute: A1/B1/C1/F2/G2; validate: A2/B2/F1/G1): A1/B1/C1/F2 judges REFUTE the access-control finding (strongest form, F2 judge: minimum_access_level('action')=='viewer' shipped in the same commit 328cc484865 that made actions access-controlled, so 'none' rows can never have existed and filter_queryset_by_access_level is a no-op for actions); A2/B2/F1 judges VALIDATE it (strongest form, F1 judge: filter_queryset_by_access_level's standard branch excludes object-level-'none'-blocked ids regardless of resource-level access, ActionViewSet includes AccessControlViewSetMixin, and the PR's own get_action enforces check_object_access, so list-vs-get is internally inconsistent). NEEDS HUMAN ADJUDICATION before anyone acts on it. Arm G added two final positions: the G1 judge VALIDATES (verified posthog/api/routing.py:369-395 applies filter_queryset_by_access_level on REST list, which the tool path lacks); the G2 judge sides with DISMISSAL on new grounds — the explicitly commented-out get_list_response_with_access_control in AccessControlViewSetMixin documents a deliberate product-wide decision not to ACL-filter list responses, making the tool's behavior intentional parity.

runs.label: C-sonnet5val-xhigh-1

old_findings.matched_title: CreateActionTool/UpdateActionTool have no length bound on `name`, so a name over 400 chars raises an unhandled DataError instead of a retryable ActionToolError — a gap the REST path doesn't have
list_actions has no lower-bound validation on limit/offset, causing a crash or silent wrong result
UpdateActionTool never treats a full steps-replacement as a dangerous operation, unlike the established UpsertDashboardTool precedent for destructive updates
No upper bound on `steps`/`properties` size for create_action/update_action, unlike the explicitly capped list_actions

new_findings.title: New action tools break the established one-icon-per-entity convention
core.py's mutating functions perform no authorization of their own — safety depends entirely on the caller remembering to check first
Check-then-act race on action name uniqueness has no DB backstop
list_actions fetches full rows including the unused bytecode/embedding columns for every page
create_action's resource-limit check adds a query but never actually bounds agent-driven creation
list_actions / get_action_object filter by `team=` object, bypassing the RootTeamQuerySet parent-team remap that writes and the name check rely on
ActionStepInput selector/tag_name/text/href are silently dropped unless event is '$autocapture', but the descriptions don't say so

runs.notes: Yardstick: 4/10 caught (3 valid + old#7 dismissed); missed 1,4,6,8,9,10 — the chunk-2 (subagent exposure) and chunk-3 (DEFAULT_TOOL_KEYS, Markdown injection) yardstick sets missed entirely, plus activity attribution. Matching caveats: old#2 catch is the length-validation half only (trim-normalization missed); the 'core.py no self-auth' finding is NOT old#4 (undocumented auth contract vs resource-editor preflight blocking object-level grants — verified _check_resource_access uses only check_access_level_for_resource, so old#4 remains a distinct miss); the .only() projection finding is NOT old#6 (DB column fetch cost vs unbounded rendered output). VALIDATOR QUALITY (sonnet-5 @ xhigh): 11 verdicts, judged 10-10.5/11 correct — every load-bearing claim spot-checked in-repo was accurate (RootTeamQuerySet remaps only the team_id kwarg with the exact TODO; no (team,name) unique constraint on Action; ActionSerializer does the same check-then-act; steps_to_expr compiles element logic only under event=='$autocapture'; is_dangerous_operation defaults False with UpsertDashboardTool precedent). Probes: old#3 correctly VALID; the access-control family is absent from this dump (judge sides with the refuters, so its absence costs nothing). The old#7 dismissal is the one arguable call (defensible precision-over-recall; forfeits a yardstick catch). NOTABLE FIRST: the validator ESCALATED the team=/team_id= environments-remap bug to must_fix — fully verified and justified; prior rounds observed the raise capability never fires. #5 caught VALID = its 2nd-ever validation (3rd-ever surface).

runs.label: F-fable5-low-1

old_findings.matched_title: Action names are stored unstripped, so whitespace variants bypass the duplicate-name check
Negative limit/offset from the LLM crashes list_actions with an unhandled exception instead of a retryable error
update_action can destructively wipe or invert an action's semantics without the approval gate that delete gets
Action descriptions are rendered untruncated in list output, weakening the context-window cap the tool is designed around
New default action tools are not added to DEFAULT_TOOL_KEYS, so the frontend 'default tools' capability listing omits them

new_findings.title: Delete approval flow fetches the action and runs the object access check twice
Unbounded action name interpolated into tool status label
Unvalidated `properties: list[dict]` can persist malformed property filters and crash save() unhandled
Stored action names/descriptions are rendered verbatim into the agent's context
list_actions exposes actions the user is denied object-level access to
Pagination ordering is not fully deterministic for unnamed actions
Delete approval flow re-runs preview kwargs after user-modified payload without revalidating action_id type
delete_action and get_action display labels never identify which action is affected
Duplicate-name check and save are not atomic — concurrent creates/updates can produce duplicate action names
Max-path action CRUD skips the `report_user_action` product analytics the REST path emits
Element-matching step fields are silently ignored unless event is "$autocapture", turning steps into match-all
Malformed LLM-supplied property filters are silently compiled to a match-everything constant

runs.notes: Matching: 5/10 old findings surfaced by root cause (2 valid, 3 dismissed), 5 missed (1,4,7,8,10). Old#2 partial — nails the unstripped-name/duplicate-bypass half (the OTHER half from every prior partial catch), omits max_length. Old#10 is a verified NEAR-MISS, not a match: dump finding 2 hits the same interpolation site but claims a cosmetic length defect and explicitly disclaims injection; judge verified the Markdown-image exfiltration vector IS real (LangGraphActivity → MarkdownMessage → LemonMarkdown.Renderer with images enabled) and the validator's 'React escapes strings' dismissal rationale is factually wrong. Volume verdict: the 25-raw record is a MIX — genuine new breadth in the valid set (5 verified novel findings incl. two silent match-all correctness bugs confirmed in posthog/hogql/property.py: element fields ignored without $autocapture, and strict=False property_to_expr degrading malformed dicts to Constant(1)) but ordinary yardstick coverage; the dismissed tail is mostly real noise (self-conceding, cosmetic, platform-wide). VALIDATOR QUALITY (fable-5 @ low): ~15/17 correct — all 7 validations correct; wrong-leaning dismissals: old#6 (the PR's own bounded-context claim doesn't hold against an unbounded TextField; old ReviewHog validated it) and debatably old#5 (delete is approval-gated in the same PR while equally destructive steps-replacement is not). Probes: old#3 correctly VALID. Access-control family: THIS judge sides with the VALIDATORS (see protocol note for the argument).

runs.label: F-fable5-low-2

old_findings.matched_title: Action name length is unvalidated — names over 400 chars raise an unhandled DataError instead of a retryable tool error
Unvalidated `limit`/`offset` can crash `list_actions` with an uncaught ValueError
New action tools missing from DEFAULT_TOOL_KEYS, so they never appear in the default tool listing UI

new_findings.title: _STEP_FIELDS filtering in to_step_dict is dead code with a misleading justification comment
list_actions bypasses object-level access controls, exposing restricted actions
Pagination ordering by name alone is non-deterministic, so pages can skip or duplicate actions
Name-uniqueness check is check-then-write with no DB constraint (race under concurrent creates)
list_actions search and ordering have no supporting index
Redis publish in post_save can fail after the row is committed, reporting failure for a successful write
Delete confirmation flow performs the action fetch + access check twice per invocation
Detailed action rendering collapses property filters to a count, so the get_action → update_action round-trip silently drops them
Malformed step property filters are silently dropped, so the created action over-matches while the tool reports success
Max tool writes skip the REST path's report_user_action product analytics events

runs.notes: Catches: 3/10 valid (#2 partial — length half; #3 probe correctly VALID; #9 exact, soft-downgraded to consider defensibly). All misses real: chunk 3 produced 0 raw issues from 3 of its 4 review units (so #10 never surfaced) and chunk 2 missed the subagent gap (#8). Root-cause rulings: the get→update property-filter round-trip-loss finding shares consequence with #5 but roots in lossy rendering, not the missing approval gate — not a catch; the list access finding is exposure-on-read, not #4's false-denial — not a catch. CALIBRATION PROBE: this judge REFUTES the access-control family with the strongest evidence yet — minimum_access_level('action')=='viewer' shipped in the SAME commit (328cc484865) that made actions access-controlled, so 'none' rows (resource- or object-level) can never have existed for actions, making filter_queryset_by_access_level a no-op for them and the claimed trigger unreachable; the validator's must_fix VALID (the run's only must_fix) is therefore judged over-permissive — hence its new_junk despite verdict_valid=true. VALIDATOR QUALITY (fable-5 @ low): 12/13 correct — all 5 dismissals correct with precise grounded argumentation (traced the ValueError to the generic except in executables.py; two defensible soft-downgrades); sole error = the contested access finding. Volume verdict: real breadth on chunk 1, not across the PR; of the 8 headline VALIDs, 7 are genuinely valid per the judge — the get→update round-trip loss and the strict=False over-match are genuinely strong new findings, both verified against posthog/hogql/property.py.

runs.label: G-sonnet5-high-1

old_findings.matched_title: No length validation on `name` before `action.save()` risks an uncaught Postgres `StringDataRightTruncation` error
list_actions has no lower-bound validation on limit/offset, letting negative values leak an unhandled Django ValueError instead of a friendly retryable error

new_findings.title: Check-then-act race on action name uniqueness (no DB constraint backing it)
DeleteActionTool re-fetches the action and re-runs the object-level access check multiple times per delete
New action tool icons break the file's established entity-icon convention (and diverge from the existing 'Search actions' icon)
Action name/description/step values are interpolated into LLM tool output with no untrusted-data framing, unlike the sibling execute_sql tool
create_action issues a full COUNT query for a limit check that's a no-op in the common case
Five new action tools added unconditionally to DEFAULT_TOOLS with no feature-flag gate, increasing per-request LLM payload for every conversation

runs.notes: Recall collapsed at high effort: only 2/10 yardstick findings caught, and the effort drop cost REAL findings, not noise — all five high-value old findings are missed (#1 activity attribution, #4 object-grant RBAC mismatch, #5 unapproved destructive step replacement, #8 subagent write exposure, #10 Markdown-render exfiltration), plus #6/#7/#9. The 11 raw findings were padded: old #3 was caught twice (the logic-correctness and blind-spots variants of the same negative-pagination root cause both survived dedup — a dedup failure), and 4 of 6 new findings are junk-class. Matching judgment calls: old #2 counted via the length-validation finding (identical lines and fix; trim half uncovered); old #6 NOT counted (injection finding's root cause is missing untrusted-data framing, not the bounded-listing claim); old #7 NOT counted (shape-validation defect, not size/sync-bytecode CPU); old #8 NOT counted — a striking near-miss that even quotes the 'DO NOT EXTEND THE TOOLKIT' warning but concludes 'add a feature flag' instead of the subagent capability-boundary defect. VALIDATOR QUALITY (sonnet-5 @ high): 10/10 verdicts correct or defensible — all 5 dismissals well-evidenced, none killed a yardstick catch; only quibble is must_fix severity on the prompt-injection keep. Probes: old #3 correctly VALID (both copies). Access-control family absent from the dump; this judge's position: VALID (verified posthog/api/routing.py:369-395 applies object-level filtering on REST list, which the tool path lacks). Net: verdict quality holds at high effort; finding DISCOVERY is what degraded.

runs.label: G-sonnet5-high-2

old_findings.matched_title: list_actions crashes on negative limit/offset and silently ignores an explicit limit=0
UpdateActionTool never requires approval even when `steps` silently destroys all existing trigger conditions
New read-only action tools are omitted from DEFAULT_TOOL_KEYS, so the UI misreports what's available in auto/plan mode

new_findings.title: ActionStepInput.properties documents a restricted contract ('event'/'person') that isn't enforced, allowing 'hogql' property filters to reach the HogQL compiler
ListActionsTool does not enforce object-level access control, unlike its sibling tools
TOOL_DEFINITIONS record doesn't actually enforce the name/description contract for new entries
_check_name_available has a check-then-create race allowing duplicate action names
Only ActionToolError is treated as recoverable — other exceptions from Action.save() surface as opaque tool failures
Name search/list filters have no supporting index at the scale the tool advertises
create_action/update_action/delete_action never fire the report_user_action analytics events that the REST path emits
New action tools break the established entity-icon convention, reusing generic operation icons instead of iconForType('action')

runs.notes: Caught 3/10 (old #3, #5, #9 — all held VALID) and missed 7, including both must_fix-grade old findings (#8 subagent exposure, #10 Markdown injection) plus #1/#2/#4/#6/#7 — the report_user_action and name-race findings are topically adjacent to old #1/#2 but have different root causes, so not counted. Verdict on the effort question: the drop from xhigh cost real high-severity findings, not just noise, though it added two net-new valid keeps (the report_user_action analytics gap — genuinely good, directly contradicts the PR's stated REST-parity claim — and the cosmetic icon-convention keep). VALIDATOR QUALITY (sonnet-5 @ high): 11/11 verdicts correct or defensible — all 6 dismissals cite verified evidence (pre-existing REST parity, commented-out list-ACL code, already-handled exceptions); the weakest keep (icons) appropriately downgraded to consider. Probes: old #3 correctly VALID. Access-control family present and DISMISSED; this judge sides with the dismissal on new evidence — the explicitly commented-out get_list_response_with_access_control in AccessControlViewSetMixin documents a deliberate product-wide decision not to ACL-filter list responses, so the tool's behavior is intentional parity (ideal disposition: a downgraded 'document why' note).
